name: website_content_crawler
title: Website Content Crawler
description: Crawl a small same-origin website slice and return clean page text, titles, final URLs, and discovered internal links for RAG, audits, and agent context.

seo.title: Website Content Crawler Tool for RAG and AI Agents
seo.description: Crawl a small same-origin website slice and return cleaned page titles, URLs, text, word counts, and internal links through Better Fetch's browser engine.
seo.intro: Website Content Crawler gives agents a controlled way to turn a public website into clean text. It starts from one URL, follows same-origin links up to a page budget, renders each page through Better Fetch, removes navigation-heavy markup, and returns pages that can be saved into RAG, audit, or monitoring workflows.

useCases.title: RAG ingestion
useCases.description: Collect a small, targeted set of website pages and transform them into normalized text before loading them into a vector store or knowledge base.

useCases.title: Site audits
useCases.description: Let an agent inspect visible titles, final URLs, word counts, and internal links without manually copying content from each page.

faqs.question: Does Website Content Crawler crawl the whole site?
faqs.answer: No. It intentionally keeps a small page budget so agent calls stay predictable. Increase max_pages only for focused same-origin crawls.

faqs.question: Does the crawler follow external links?
faqs.answer: No. It keeps traversal on the same origin as the starting URL and records external links only as page text or markup context when visible.

max_chars_per_page.description: Truncate each extracted page text to this many characters

include_path_prefixes.description: Optional path prefixes to follow, e.g. /docs or /blog

links.description: Number of same-origin links discovered on this page
